tokens: (Pause) Man, am I going to miss one-sixth g.
Soon as I can get the switches on here.
(Pause) Okay, Joe, now you're going to have to say again where you want us to go, because...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Just north, Dave, along the side of the rim.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I thought you said something about 3/10ths of a click, didn't you?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll tell you what, we were...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...via the easiest route north, and all we need is the photography from the point where you stop.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Tell you when we get to 2.8, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...what you're picking up is just the base for the stereo photography.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: It's fairly easy driving here, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It's a lot easier than the other place.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You don't want me to run us over that big one there, do you?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Take it easy on the old Rover.
I'll get a good trade-in value on it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: How much farther we got to go?
I got to plan where we're heading here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: About another 2/10ths to go; I'm reading...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Maybe up by that large block at 12 o'clock (meaning directly ahead of the Rover).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Gee, the one with the great big vesicles in it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, notice that fresh one (almost certainly means a fresh crater) that's just this side of it?
It looks like a light colored - almost a yellow - ray that extends to the west of it?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...very fresh crater right on the rim of the...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: This is one of the Twins!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Man, we're right at it, and it's a deep fella.
(Pause) Keep a little momentum going here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) There's a flat part over there to the left.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Look at that great vesicular (rock) there.
Guess what we just lost again: the front steering.
Oh, I know what('s wrong) There.
Turn the switch on; it works a lot better.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me get to this level spot over here.
Okay, up on the rim of the Twin there would be a great place to take a pan.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Either that or over on those rocks over at 11 o'clock.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Do the rim of the Twin there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Why don't you read the coordinates, partner?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And thinking downstream, here, all we need is photography from this stop, and we're looking towards arriving back at the LM in about 45 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: No time to go to the Northern Complex, huh?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Hey, Jim, up on the rim.
Right over here on the rim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Then you get the crater and you can get all over the place.
Then I can take the 500 from up there, too.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It has debris on the order of, oh, a foot or so almost throughout.
No accumulation of talus at the bottom; and it's got fines covering everything, nothing really sharply exposed.
And most of the fragments are subangular and it looks like non-vesicular, although I do see one highly vesicular one right in the bottom.
And it's about 60 meters across and maybe, oh, 10 meters deep, smooth sides, and a very slightly raised rim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, as craters go around here, it's deep.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Jim, are you taking your pan, now?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Beautiful, and maybe you could give us a hand...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...moving a little bit to the north.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Dave laughs) Looks like you're looking back at Earth.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yes, sir, and our necks are killing...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, there's a large block there just to the north of that, Dave.
It looks like it might have a contact in it - between a dark, very vesicular basalt and that light-colored tan.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Dave, are you firing off the big camera?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I've got an angular fragment here, subangular, about 4 feet by 5 feet; and the vesicles that are facing to the southwest are large vesicles, about 3 inches, 2 to 3 inches in diameter....
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: The Rover's not stressed for it.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...gradual transition...(Laughing at Joe's joke) Oh, I'd love to bring it back.
I guess I'll just take some close-ups here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh, you got your thing, yeah.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I don't have my scoop; I'll estimate...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Just to the north of the large one I just mentioned, there're two other large fragments.
And there's a fracture right between them, and they also have the large-vesicle pattern.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Don't know whether we've sampled this (rock-type).
And the material that has the large vesicles has long laths of probably plagioclase.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, a long lath's about a centimeter.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And as much as we hate to, we're going to have to get you aboard the Rover, heading back across the mare towards the east, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Getting ready to move out, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Long Pause) Leave something here for the next guy.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, hop on, and we'll get on with it here.
(Pause) Get your ol' handy dandy seatbelt fixed up there because we'll be going all the way back.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And TV camera'll be (pointed) aft and down.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I knocked that seatbelt off, Dave, I'll have to get off and get it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Why don't you let me get it and hold it.
(Pause) Too much back, Jim; you go back too much.
You're a tad too far back when you get on it.
transcript.speaker: Scott
